# AC-05: Bias & Fairness Testing

**Track:** AI Controls (`AC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

For changes involving AI systems: test for discriminatory outcomes across protected characteristics (e.g. age, gender, ethnicity, disability) as defined by applicable EU regulations. Results feed into RC-05. Not applicable to changes with no AI component.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 4 | Testing & Documentation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent executes, human reviews |
| **Agent does** | Execute bias and fairness test suite across protected characteristic groups; measure disparate impact. |
| **Human does** | Review bias test results; determine if disparate impact is within acceptable thresholds. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Bias & fairness test report | evidence | Test results showing disparate impact measurements across protected groups with threshold comparison. | 7 years (AI Act) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | AI Governance Lead |

Discriminatory outcomes exceeding defined thresholds block deployment. Design or training data must be revised.

## Dependencies

- `AC-01`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| EU AI Act | Art. 10(2)(f) | Examination of training data and outputs for potential biases |
| EU AI Act | Art. 10(5) | Special category data may be used for bias detection and correction |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(1) | Appropriate levels of accuracy across all population groups |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** AI Governance · **Owner role:** AI Governance Lead
